head.hi: The Minister in China (Johnson) to the Secretary of State

div.p:
92. Following from American Consul General at Mukden:
“January 25, noon. All available information indicates that drive on Jehol has not been canceled but that preparations therefor are not yet completed. February or early part of March is regarded
in well-informed circles as the best season for the drive. It is understood that major disposition of troops at vantage points has already taken place.
Seven heavy bombers bombarded volunteers in the vicinity of Kailu on the 23d. On the same day bandit clearing operations started in the Liaohsi area southwest of Mukden in which Manchukuo troops reenforced with Japanese units are being used.”
